content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription is a literal "TODO" placeholder (Info.plist) and NSCameraUsageDescription is generic boilerplate not tied to the document-scan feature seen in ScannerView.swift.

Pierre: Apple rejects purpose strings that are placeholders or too generic to explain why the permission is needed, and "TODO" will almost certainly trigger a rejection since it's not even valid copy — also no location usage is visible anywhere in the code, so this entry may be unused and should be removed if location isn't actually used. The camera string should be rewritten to something specific like "SnapScan uses your camera to scan and digitize documents," matching the actual feature in ScannerView.swift.
